name: Scottsburg Building And Loan Association
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank chartered in 1889, offering personal and business checking, savings, mortgages, and home loans to Scott County, Indiana residents.
description_long: Scottsburg Building and Loan Association was incorporated on August 8, 1889, making it the oldest financial institution in Scott County, Indiana. The institution was founded to meet the growing need for home financing in the Scott County area during a period of expansion. Over 130 years later, the association continues to operate as a traditional community bank serving personal and business customers in the region. The bank offers a comprehensive range of deposit products including personal checking accounts, savings accounts, and Certificates of Deposit (CDs), as well as Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs). On the lending side, Scottsburg provides home loans, mortgage loans, construction loans, home improvement financing, and home equity lines of credit. The bank also serves business customers with dedicated business checking and savings accounts, plus business lending products. Additional services include online banking, mobile banking, credit cards, wire transfers, and cybersecurity education resources. As an FDIC-insured institution "backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government," Scottsburg distinguishes itself through its long institutional history and deep community roots spanning 130+ years. The bank emphasizes its commitment to local service and recently redesigned its website and online banking platform to improve digital accessibility across desktop and mobile devices. Their tagline "When You Grow, We Grow" reflects a community-focused philosophy rather than a large-scale commercial orientation. Scottsburg Building and Loan appears to be a stable, traditional community bank best suited for customers seeking personalized service and long-term relationships with a locally-rooted institution. However, as a regional bank, it may have limited product innovation and competitive rates compared to larger national banks or online-only institutions. The website does not provide specific rate information, fee schedules, or detailed product terms, requiring direct customer contact for detailed comparisons.
services: Cybersecurity education and identity theft resources
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full U.S. government protection
130+ years of community banking history since 1889, establishing long-term stability
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, mortgages, construction loans, and home equity lines of credit
Dedicated business banking services with separate checking and savings accounts for commercial customers
Recently redesigned website and online banking platform optimized for desktop and mobile device access
Mobile banking option available for account access and management on-the-go
Equal Housing Lender commitment with transparent community involvement
cons: No rate, fee, or APR information publicly available on website—requires calling (812) 752-3230 for quotes
Limited geographic footprint as regional Scott County bank may restrict convenience and accessibility for non-local customers
Website lacks detailed product comparison tools, calculators, or product-specific terms and conditions
No mention of modern fintech features, automated loan approval processes, or competitive digital-first capabilities
Minimal transparency on minimum deposit requirements, account minimums, or qualification criteria for lending products
best_for: Scott County, Indiana residents seeking a locally-rooted bank with 130+ years of community history
Homebuyers and homeowners looking for mortgage, construction, and home equity financing from a traditional lender
Small business owners in the region needing personalized business banking and lending relationships
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance and stability over cutting-edge digital features or competitive rates
diagnosis: Scottsburg Building and Loan Association is best for Indiana residents in Scott County seeking traditional community banking with 130+ years of stability, FDIC insurance, and personalized service for mortgages and home lending. The main caveat is that the bank does not publicly display rates, fees, or competitive product details online, requiring direct contact to evaluate pricing and may lack modern digital conveniences offered by larger national or online-only banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
